Commercial Linkage Fees (CLF) are a standard tool used by local communities for generating affordable housing resources. The CLF is similar to other impact fees levied on new development, and helps cover the cost associated with creating new or expanded public facilities to meet the additional demand created by the development, such as parks, schools, libraries and streets. Before levying an impact fee, jurisdictions are required by state law to complete a nexus study that shows the linkage between the new development and the increased demand for the facilities. The results of the nexus study establish the maximum legal fee that may be charged.

manual_audit_notes.llm_analysis_excerpt: The San Jose Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) is a one-time impact fee levied on commercial development to fund affordable housing for Extremely Low through Moderate Income households. Implemented on September 1, 2020, the fee applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects that add gross floor area or change building use. While the rates are determined by four geographic subareas and adjusted annually by the Engineering News Record (ENR) Construction Cost Index, the specific dollar amounts are not listed in the source text.
manual_audit_notes.llm_quality_note: LLM analysis produced a substantive answer from persisted evidence.
manual_audit_notes.reader_quality_note: Reader output was persisted and provided to analysis.

fail_closed_reasons: analysis_sufficiency:Partially sufficient. The evidence provides the fee structure, effective dates, and project categories, but lacks the specific fee rates (dollar amounts) and any information regarding the pass-through of costs to household cost of living.

key_points: Affected project categories include new and existing Non-Residential Projects that add gross floor area or change existing building use.
The direct development-cost mechanism involves a one-time impact fee based on four geographic subareas, with rates adjusted annually via the Engineering News Record (ENR) Construction Cost Index.
Specific fee rates are not provided in the evidence; they are established in a separate Fee Resolution.
Secondary research is required to analyze pass-through to household cost of living as the provided text does not address economic impacts on residents.
analysis.sufficiency_state: Partially sufficient. The evidence provides the fee structure, effective dates, and project categories, but lacks the specific fee rates (dollar amounts) and any information regarding the pass-through of costs to household cost of living.
analysis.summary: The San Jose Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) is a one-time impact fee levied on commercial development to fund affordable housing for Extremely Low through Moderate Income households. Implemented on September 1, 2020, the fee applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects that add gross floor area or change building use. While the rates are determined by four geographic subareas and adjusted annually by the Engineering News Record (ENR) Construction Cost Index, the specific dollar amounts are not listed in the source text.

selected_chunks.snippet: The Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) is an impact fee levied on commercial development for the provision of affordable housing.

The production and preservation of affordable housing is a priority for City Council. Commercial Linkage Fee funds will be used to facilitate the production of Affordable Housing for Extremely Low, Ve
The San José City Council voted on September 1, 2020 to begin charging commercial developers fees to fund affordable housing.
The Commercial Linkage Fee is a one-time impact fee that applies to new and existing Non-Residential Projects in San José that either add gross floor area or change the existing building use

Fees are based on the 4 geographic subareas of the City and their ability to sustain the CLF fee. The rates are established in the Fee Resolution and are increased by the Engineering News Record (ENR)
- Proposed Changes to Mobilehome Rent Ordinance

- Proposed Changes to Inclusionary Housing Ordinance

top_results.snippet: The city’s consultant, Keyser Marston Associates, found that a fee of up to $176 per square foot for new retail buildings would “reflect the cost of mitigating affordable housing impacts of new development.” The consultant identified $151 per square foot as the reflective cost of new high-tech office buildings. “Our business is already a tenuous situation… I don’t think that this is the time to increase fees for commercial (developers) and job creators in the city of San Jose.”. “Our business is already a tenuous situation… I don’t think that this is the time to increase fees for commercial (developers) and job creators in the city of San Jose.”. Literally, other cities have millions from residential AND commercial fees – and San Jose has already lost out on tens of millions that could actually make more Measure A projects viable…or more affordable housing generally.
top_results.title: After years of debate, San Jose releases study on commercial development fees - San José Spotlight
